subject: Public Health
title: Sanitation and the Environment
description: A quick win: understand Sanitation and the Environment.

hook.text: Human waste isn't just a health problem—it's an environmental disaster polluting waterways worldwide.

buildup.text: Untreated sewage causes algal blooms that suffocate fish and destroy aquatic ecosystems.

discovery.text: Nutrient pollution from human waste creates ocean dead zones—areas where nothing can survive.

twist.text: Human waste can be a resource—treated sewage sludge provides fertilizer, biogas, and reclaimed water.

climax.text: Circular sanitation—recovering resources from waste—turns a pollution problem into an economic opportunity.

punchline.text: Waste is only waste if you don't recover its value.

quiz.question: What useful resources can be recovered from treated sewage?
